skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: There are certain units, which are accepted for use with the SI. It includes units which are in continuous everyday use, in particular the traditional units of time and of angle, together with a few other units which have assumed increasing technical importance. There are also units which are currently accepted for use with the SI to satisfy the needs of commercial, legal and specialist scientific interests or are important for the interpretation of older texts.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: 1⁄100 of a chain and 1⁄1000 of a furlong.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derived unit of luminance.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: The rod is also called pole or perch and equal to 5 1⁄2 yards

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derived unit of Area
The SI derived unit of specific volume.
The SI derived unit for the refractive index.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: Defined as exactly 0.9144 metres by the International yard and pound agreement of 1959
1/12000 of a foot or 25.4 μm. Also known as mil
Traditionally 3 miles. No longer an official unit in any nation.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derived unit for density or mass density.
The SI derived unit of volume.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: One tenth of a nautical mile. Equal to 100 fathoms under the strict definition.
The British Admiralty in practice used a fathom of 6 feet. This was despite its being 1⁄1000 of a nautical mile (i.e. 6.08 feet) until the adoption of the international nautical mile.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: A unit of weight in the avoirdupois system equal to 2,240 pounds (1,016.05 kg) in Britain (the long ton) and 2,000 pounds (907.18 kg) in the United States (the short ton). As a unit of volume it is equivalent to 100 cubic feet (the register ton) or 40 cubic feet (the freight or measurement ton)

skos:Concept.skos:definition: 100 links, 4 rods or 1⁄10 of a furlong. The distance between the two wickets on a cricket pitch.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derived unit of concentration (of amount of a substance).
The SI derived unit of speed or velocity
The SI derived unit for current density.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: Although the proper term is square rod, for centuries this unit has been called a pole or perch or, more properly, square pole or square perch.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derviced unit for wavenumber
The SI derived unit of magnetic field strength.

skos:Concept.skos:definition: Used for measuring distances at sea. Until the adoption of the international definition of 1852 metres in 1970, the British nautical (Admiralty) mile was defined as 6080 feet.

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: The SI derived unit for acceleration
